mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate wage income, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS fail to coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A restrictive federal funds rate above 5% increases the cost of capital, suppresses corporate investment and equity valuations through higher discount rates, and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising retail sales and falling industrial production generate a demand-supply imbalance that squeezes corporate margins and profitability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises month-over-month and INDPRO falls month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising RSAFS and falling INDPRO over two months does not predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In an elevated inflation regime, a rising unemployment rate signals easing wage-price pressure and reduces the expected path of monetary tightening, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 180 and UNRATE rises month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in real retail sales signal weakening consumer demand and deteriorating corporate revenue growth expectations, which drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Declines in RSAFS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month acceleration in industrial production above 1.5% indicates improving manufacturing capacity utilization and margin expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises more than 1.5% over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts exceeding 10% from its 12-month high signals contraction in residential investment and construction employment, dragging down aggregate demand and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls more than 10% from its 12-month high, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals an easing monetary cycle that lowers corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS declines by more than 25bp over 3 months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
